qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Hash-navigation fragment selector crash causes client-side DoS
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets `hashNavigation` selector construction from `document.location.hash` in `src/modules/hash-navigation/hash-navigation.mjs`, while the advisory describes a different root cause: prototype pollution through `swiper.default.extendDefaults()` when the forbidden-key check in `src/shared/utils.mjs` is bypassed by tampering with `Array.prototype.indexOf` and passing a `__proto__` payload.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's prototype-pollution root cause in `extendDefaults()` or the `Array.prototype.indexOf` denylist bypass described in GHSA-hmx5-qpq5-p643.
evidence: The advisory describes prototype pollution via `swiper.default.extendDefaults(JSON.parse('{"__proto__":{"polluted":"yes"}}'))` after overriding `Array.prototype.indexOf`, which defeats the forbidden-key check in `src/shared/utils.mjs`.

The only final qualifying finding is `hash_navigation_selector_dos`, whose report and PoC are about an uncaught `SyntaxError` from selector construction in `src/modules/hash-navigation/hash-navigation.mjs` when a crafted URL fragment such as `#\\` reaches `Element.matches()`.

The session manifest under `.judge/input_manifest.json` lists only `hash_navigation_selector_dos` as a final qualifying finding, so there is no qualifying report for the advisory's `extendDefaults` prototype-pollution path.
